name: app.R
content:
#
# This is a Shiny web application. You can run the application by clicking
# the 'Run App' button above.
#
# Find out more about building applications with Shiny here:
#
#    https://shiny.posit.co/
#

library(tidyverse)
library(tidytext)
library(shiny)

# Trash Proportions Precomputed with:
#######################################
#trashwheel <- readr::read_csv('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/main/data/2024/2024-03-05/trashwheel.csv') %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ replace_na(.x, 0)))
#trash.prop <- trashwheel %>% 
#  select(Name, Date, Dumpster, PlasticBottles, Polystyrene, CigaretteButts, GlassBottles, PlasticBags, Wrappers, SportsBalls) %>% 
#  mutate(total = PlasticBottles + Polystyrene + CigaretteButts + GlassBottles + PlasticBags + Wrappers + SportsBalls) %>% 
#  mutate(PlasticBottles = PlasticBottles / total, Polystyrene = Polystyrene / total, CigaretteButts = CigaretteButts / total, GlassBottles = GlassBottles / total, PlasticBags = PlasticBags / total, Wrappers = Wrappers / total, SportsBalls = SportsBalls / total) %>% 
#  select(Name, PlasticBottles, Polystyrene, CigaretteButts, GlassBottles, PlasticBags, Wrappers, SportsBalls) %>% 
#  na.omit()

trash.prop <- readr::read_csv("trash_prop.csv")

pca_data <- trash.prop %>%
  select(-Name)

pca_model <- prcomp(pca_data, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)

pca_scores <- as.data.frame(pca_model$x) %>%
  mutate(Name = trash.prop$Name)

pca_loadings <- as.data.frame(pca_model$rotation) %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column("terms") %>%
  pivot_longer(-terms, names_to = "component", values_to = "value")

pca1 <- pca_scores %>%
  ggplot(aes(PC1, PC2)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = Name), alpha = 0.7, size = 2) +
  labs(color = NULL,
       title = "PCA Analysis") +
  theme(axis.ticks = element_blank(),
        axis.text = element_blank())


pca2 <- pca_loadings %>%
  filter(component %in% c("PC1", "PC2")) %>%
  group_by(component) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = abs(value), n = 8) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(terms = tidytext::reorder_within(terms, abs(value), component)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(abs(value), terms, fill = value > 0)) +
  geom_col() +
  facet_wrap(~component, scales = "free_y") +
  tidytext::scale_y_reordered() +
  labs(
    x = "Absolute value of contribution",
    y = NULL,
    fill = "Positive?",
    title = "Principal Components Analysis"
  )

#UMAP Data Precomputed with this code:
######################################
#library(uwot)
#
#umap_coords <- umap(
#  trash.prop %>% select(-Name),
#  scale = TRUE
#)
#
#umap_df <- trash.prop %>%
#  mutate(
#    UMAP1 = umap_coords[,1],
#    UMAP2 = umap_coords[,2]
#  )

trash_umap <- readr::read_csv("trash_umap_precomputed.csv")

umap1 <- trash_umap %>%
  ggplot(aes(UMAP1, UMAP2)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = Name), alpha = 0.7, size = 2) +
  labs(title = "UMAP Analysis", color = NULL) +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank()
  )

ui <- fluidPage(
  textOutput("text_overall"),
  br(),
  plotOutput("plot_umap"),
  textOutput("text_umap"),
  br(),
  plotOutput("plot_pca"),
  textOutput("text_pca"),
  br(),
  plotOutput("plot_pca_bar"),
  textOutput("text_pca_bar")
  
)

server <- function(input, output, session) {
  output$text_overall <- renderText("In this app, we will explore whether or not the proportion of trash picked up by 4 different \"Mr. Trash Wheels\" can point us to which one is picking up the trash.")
  output$plot_umap <- renderPlot(umap1)
  output$text_umap <- renderText("This UMAP plot shows that there seems to be a connection between the specific trash collector and the proportion of trash that they deposit. There does seem to be some overlap, but overall--especially for the 'Mister Trash Wheel' trash collector--we can see clear clusters in the data.")
  output$plot_pca <- renderPlot(pca1)
  output$text_pca <- renderText("We can also run a PCA analysis on the data to see if we can find similar clustering and use the PCs to determine specific trash proportions that could be leading to the clusters. From this graph, we can see that there is some level of clustering, though much less than the UMAP graph, but we can still see the clustors, and it also helps to show the areas that were overlaping. By looking at the specific PCs, we can look as to what may be causing overlaps.")
  output$plot_pca_bar <- renderPlot(pca2)
  output$text_pca_bar <- renderText("From the PCs that we are graphing, we can see that the biggest difference in the PCs is the fact that PC1 favors the Plastic Bottle proportion, while the second favors Plastic Bags. The next big difference is the Glass Bottle and Sports Ball proportions are oppositely affecting the data. From this, we can conclude some differences that are causing the clustering.")
  
}

shinyApp(ui, server)
